description: The CNC protects civil nuclear sites and nuclear materials in England and Scotland. We also police and protect other, non-nuclear critical infrastructure. CNC is part of the Civil Nuclear Police Authority .

details.body:
The CNC protects civil nuclear sites and nuclear materials in England and Scotland. We also police and protect other, non-nuclear critical infrastructure.

CNC is part of the Civil Nuclear Police Authority.

ordered_featured_documents.summary: The UK National Threat Level has been raised to SEVERE, meaning an attack is highly likely.
ordered_featured_documents.title: UK National Threat Level raised to SEVERE

image.alt_text: Erin Kyle-Davidson, Senior Solicitor, and Doug North, Health and Wellbeing Advisor.

ordered_featured_documents.summary: The CNC’s Occupational Health, Safety and Wellbeing (OHSW) team have launched a six-month lifestyle support programme to help staff to improve their health.
ordered_featured_documents.title: CNC launch Live Well programme

image.alt_text: Nayan Mesuria, Solicitor and Insurance Manager for the CNC, during the mock trial.

ordered_featured_documents.summary: The CNC’s Legal Services team worked with leading barristers and legal professionals to deliver a a civil mock trial as a unique training opportunity.
ordered_featured_documents.title: CNC leads multi-force legal mock trial event

ordered_featured_documents.summary: The Civil Nuclear Constabulary (CNC) has published its Annual Business Plan for the coming year.
ordered_featured_documents.title: An ambitious year ahead – CNC publishes Annual Business Plan

ordered_featured_documents.summary: The CNC celebrates International Women’s Day 2026, highlighting women’s leadership, inclusive culture, and record female firearms officer numbers.
ordered_featured_documents.title: CNC Celebrates Women Across Policing on International Women’s Day

ordered_featured_documents.summary: The CNC has opened AFO recruitment at Sellafield as part of a rolling programme to sustain armed protection at one of the UK’s most sensitive nuclear sites.
ordered_featured_documents.title: Sellafield recruitment opens for Authorised Firearms Officers

details.secondary_corporate_information_pages: Read about the types of information we routinely publish in our Publication scheme. Find out about our commitment to publishing in Welsh. Our Personal information charter explains how we treat your personal information.

details.body:
• responsible for operational performance of the CNC

  • Accounting Officer for the Civil Nuclear Police Authority and CNC, ensuring demonstrable financial efficiency and effectiveness

  • improves, develops and maintains effective strategic partnerships with the CNPA, site license companies, the Office for Nuclear Regulation (Civil Nuclear Security), Home Office and Police Scotland forces and the Ministry of Defence Police

role_appointments.title: Simon Chesterman QPM - Chief Constable / Chief Executive Officer

details.body:
The Deputy Chief Constable of the CNC is responsible to the Chief Constable for developing and maintaining the capacity and capability of the CNC to deliver its mission, and:

  • to develop operational policy and practice to national standards
  • to maintaining operational interoperability with Home Office police forces and other agencies
  • to recruit, retain and develop a capable workforce with the right values, leadership and skills
  • to deputise for the Chief Constable

role_appointments.title: Simon Chesterman QPM - Deputy Chief Constable

details.body:
The Deputy Chief Constable of the CNC is responsible to the Chief Constable for developing and maintaining the capacity and capability of the CNC to deliver its mission, and:

  • to develop operational policy and practice to national standards
  • to maintaining operational interoperability with Home Office police forces and other agencies
  • to recruit, retain and develop a capable workforce with the right values, leadership and skills
  • to deputise for the Chief Constable

The Assistant Chief Constable for Professionalism is responsible for driving initiatives that uphold trust, accountability, and ethical standards while fostering an inclusive and professional working environment.

Responsibilities include providing strategic leadership for:

  • Professional Standards (PSD)
  • Vetting
  • Equality, Diversity and Inclusion (EDI)
  • Inspections
  • Culture

role_appointments.title: Kerry Smith - Assistant Chief Constable, Professionalism

details.body:
The director is responsible for:

  • estates and facilities management
  • portfolio management, programme and project delivery
  • sustainability
  • frontline logistics and recruitment
  • scheduling and deployment

role.title: Director of Corporate Services (job share), DVSA

role_appointments.title: Kerry Smith - Director of Corporate Services (job share), DVSA

details.body:
The Assistant Chief Constable, Operations, is the chief officer for all operational deployments, armed or otherwise. Responsibilities include:

  • Constabulary firearms lead on matters pertaining to the use of all weapons systems in the CNC including the deployments of the strategic escort group
  • Senior Responsible Owner (SRO) for Operational projects
  • Chairs the CNC’s firearms standing committee

The Assistant Chief Constable, Operations, is the chief officer for all operational deployments, armed or otherwise. Responsibilities include:

  • Constabulary firearms lead on matters pertaining to the use of all weapons systems in the CNC including the deployments of the strategic escort group
  • Senior Responsible Owner (SRO) for Operational projects
  • Chairs the CNC’s firearms standing committee

The post of Director of Governance, Compliance and Legal Services oversees:

  • Governance and Assurance including the CNPA committees and Chief’s Management groups as well as Portfolio Assurance for programmes, projects via the Constabulary Change Board

  • Information Governance including Data Protection, Freedom of Information and associated requirements via the IRG and IGC reporting to the SIRO

  • Legal Services covering claims and tribunals and professional legal advice

role.title: Director of Governance, Compliance and Legal Services

role_appointments.title: Richard Cawdron - Director of Governance, Compliance and Legal Services

details.description: The opening hours of the press office are 8am to 5pm, Monday to Friday. If it is outside of these times, please email.
details.title: Press enquiries

details.description:
To report suspicious activity or an incident, call 101. If a crime is in progress or there is a risk to life, call 999

Call the police anti-terrorist hotline on 0800 789 321 to report any threats to national security
details.title: Emergency contact

details.description: If you have a complaint about the Civil Nuclear Constabulary please visit our complaints page or email us

post_addresses.street_address: Civil Nuclear Constabulary Culham Campus Abingdon Oxfordshire OX14 3DB
post_addresses.title: Professional Standards Department

details.title: Make a complaint

details.description: Please note, the CNC is a specialist national infrastructure police force responsible for protecting nuclear power stations and material and as such does not investigate crime, routinely make arrests or deal with missing people cases. Please do not submit FOI requests of this nature to the CNC as they will generally have a nil response.

post_addresses.street_address: Civil Nuclear Constabulary F6 Culham Campus
post_addresses.title: Disclosures Officer

details.title: Make an FOI request

details.body:
The ACC for the Corporate Services portfolio oversees:

  • procurement
  • estates
  • information technology
  • Business and People Support System

• responsible for operational performance of the CNC

  • Accounting Officer for the Civil Nuclear Police Authority and CNC, ensuring demonstrable financial efficiency and effectiveness

  • improves, develops and maintains effective strategic partnerships with the CNPA, site license companies, the Office for Nuclear Regulation (Civil Nuclear Security), Home Office and Police Scotland forces and the Ministry of Defence Police

The Assistant Chief Constable, Operations, is the chief officer for all operational deployments, armed or otherwise. Responsibilities include:

  • Constabulary firearms lead on matters pertaining to the use of all weapons systems in the CNC including the deployments of the strategic escort group
  • Senior Responsible Owner (SRO) for Operational projects
  • Chairs the CNC’s firearms standing committee

The Deputy Chief Constable of the CNC is responsible to the Chief Constable for developing and maintaining the capacity and capability of the CNC to deliver its mission, and:

  • to develop operational policy and practice to national standards
  • to maintaining operational interoperability with Home Office police forces and other agencies
  • to recruit, retain and develop a capable workforce with the right values, leadership and skills
  • to deputise for the Chief Constable

The temporary assistant chief constable of the CNC People Programme has responsibility for delivering the programme objectives to ensure the Constabulary has sufficient, capable, motivated people.

Initial focus of the programme is on:

  • the modernisation of terms and conditions of employment

  • pension reform

  • the introduction of fitness standards

  • enhanced performance and talent management across the CNC.

The Director of People is:

  • Responsible for the provision of strategic leadership and direction for all people related matters including Human Resources, Corporate Learning and Development, Organisational Development, and People Change activity
  • The lead for the development and implementation of the People Strategy
  • To lead the development and implementation of the Health, Safety Occupational Health and Wellbeing activity
  • The strategic business partner to the Chief Constable and the Executive Team on people matters

The post of Director of Governance, Compliance and Legal Services oversees:

  • Governance and Assurance including the CNPA committees and Chief’s Management groups as well as Portfolio Assurance for programmes, projects via the Constabulary Change Board

  • Information Governance including Data Protection, Freedom of Information and associated requirements via the IRG and IGC reporting to the SIRO

  • Legal Services covering claims and tribunals and professional legal advice
